books.desc: Something in you is gone, and no one named it a loss. understand why this keeps happening to me.
books.long_desc:
Something in you is gone, and no one named it a loss.

This book is for working parent of two kids under 8s who the body is doing something and I don't understand why. It doesn't promise to make the feeling stop. It names what's happening in language you can use the next time it happens.

Chapter by chapter, you'll work with the quiet grief of a self that's been spent, and how absence keeps making itself present. Not through exposure or worksheets or morning routines — through recognition, descent, and integration. The practice the book asks for can happen on a subway, between meetings, or at 3am.

You'll walk away knowing what your body is doing and why it makes sense. Not fixed. Not healed in any final sense. But no longer alone with the pattern.

Book 7 of the Stillness Press anxiety series.
